{% extends "base.html" %} {% load i18n %} {% block title %}{% translate "Deployment" %}{% endblock %} {% block breadcrumb %}
  • {% translate "Documentation" %}
  • {% translate "Deployment" %}
    • {% endblock %} {% block content %}
    {% translate "On This Page" %}
    • {% translate "Prerequisites" %}
    • {% translate "Setup and Deploy" %}
    • {% translate "Automatic Deployment" %}
    • {% translate "Troubleshooting" %}
    • {% translate "Requirements Files" %}
    • {% translate "Deployment Scripts" %}

    {% translate "Deployment" %}

    {% translate "This template is optimized for PythonAnywhere deployment with automated scripts." %}

    {% translate "Looking for Docker?" %} {% translate "See the" %} {% translate "Docker Deployment" %} {% translate "guide for containerized deployment." %}

    {% translate "Prerequisites" %}

    • {% translate "PythonAnywhere account" %}
    • {% translate "Git repository hosting your project (GitHub, GitLab, etc.)" %}
    • {% translate "API token from PythonAnywhere account" %}

    {% translate "Getting Your API Token" %}

    1. {% translate "Log in to" %} PythonAnywhere
    2. {% translate "Click your username in the top-right corner" %}
    3. {% translate "Select" %} Account
    4. {% translate "Go to the" %} API token {% translate "tab" %}
    5. {% translate "Click" %} Create a new API token

    {% translate "Once created, the API token will be automatically available in your Bash console as the" %} API_TOKEN {% translate "environment variable." %}

    {% translate "About $USER:" %} {% translate "In the commands below," %} $USER {% translate "is a shell variable that automatically contains your PythonAnywhere username. You don't need to replace it manually." %}

    {% translate "Setup and Deploy" %}

    {% translate "Open a new Bash console in PythonAnywhere by clicking" %} Dashboard → consoles → Bash. {% translate "Then run these commands in order:" %}

    1. {% translate "Clone Your Repository" %}

    {% translate "Replace" %} <URL> {% translate "with your git repository URL:" %}

    git clone <URL> $USER.pythonanywhere.com

    2. {% translate "Navigate to Project Directory" %}

    cd $USER.pythonanywhere.com

    3. {% translate "Create Virtual Environment" %}

    {% translate "This creates a virtual environment at" %} ~/.virtualenvs/$USER:

    mkvirtualenv $USER --python=/usr/bin/python3.13

    4. {% translate "Upgrade pip" %}

    pip install --upgrade pip

    5. {% translate "Install Dependencies" %}

    pip install -r requirements/production.txt

    6. {% translate "Run Setup Script" %}

    python scripts/setup_pythonanywhere.py

    {% translate "This script will automatically:" %}

    • {% translate "Create the web app on PythonAnywhere" %}
    • {% translate "Configure static file mappings (/static/, /media/, etc.)" %}
    • {% translate "Set up environment variables (.env file)" %}
    • {% translate "Run database migrations" %}
    • {% translate "Collect static files" %}
    • {% translate "Create a superuser (admin account)" %}
    • {% translate "Generate and configure WSGI file" %}
    • {% translate "Enable HTTPS force" %}
    {% translate "Important:" %} {% translate "The setup script creates your project at" %} /home/$USER/$USER.pythonanywhere.com {% translate "and registers" %} $USER.pythonanywhere.com {% translate "as your web app domain. This is the expected behavior." %}

    {% translate "Automatic Deployment" %}

    {% translate "For future updates, run the update script from your project directory:" %}

    cd ~/$USER.pythonanywhere.com
./scripts/update.sh

    {% translate "The update script will:" %}

    • {% translate "Backup the database to" %} ~/.backups/
    • {% translate "Pull latest changes from git" %}
    • {% translate "Install/upgrade dependencies" %}
    • {% translate "Run database migrations" %}
    • {% translate "Collect static files" %}
    • {% translate "Reload the web app" %}

    {% translate "Backup Database Only" %}

    {% translate "If you want to backup without updating:" %}

    ./scripts/backupdb.sh

    {% translate "Troubleshooting" %}

    {% translate "Verify your API token is set:" %}

    echo $API_TOKEN

    {% translate "If empty, regenerate your token in Account → API token tab." %}

    {% translate "Make sure your virtualenv is activated (required before running Django commands):" %}

    workon $USER

    {% translate "Or manually activate:" %}

    source ~/.virtualenvs/$USER/bin/activate

    • {% translate "Check error log in Web tab" %}
    • {% translate "Verify .env file exists and has correct values" %}
    • {% translate "Check that virtualenv path is correct" %}
    • {% translate "Ensure static files are collected" %}

    {% translate "Requirements Files" %}

    {% translate "File" %} {% translate "Use Case" %}
    requirements/base.txt {% translate "All environments (Django, allauth, PWA, etc.)" %}
    requirements/development.txt {% translate "Local development (+ debug toolbar)" %}
    requirements/production.txt {% translate "Production server (base.txt + pythonanywhere-core)" %}

    {% translate "Deployment Scripts" %}

    {% translate "All scripts are located in the" %} scripts/ {% translate "directory:" %}

    {% translate "Script" %} {% translate "Purpose" %}
    setup_pythonanywhere.py {% translate "Initial PythonAnywhere setup (creates webapp, runs migrations, creates superuser)" %}
    update.sh {% translate "Full deployment update (backup, pull, install, migrate, collectstatic, reload)" %}
    reload.sh {% translate "Touch WSGI file to reload server without other changes" %}
    backupdb.sh {% translate "Database backup to ~/.backups/" %}
    create_wsgi.sh {% translate "Generate PythonAnywhere WSGI file" %}
    test {% translate "Run tests with test settings" %}
    push {% translate "Push to remote with submodule updates" %}
    {% endblock %}
